MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-31-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Josette D. Johnson of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / moving party Pneumo Abex LLC successor in interest to Abex Corporation erroneously sued herein as Pneumo-Abex Corporation
J. Bruce Jackson of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (510-844-5500), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Mattie McFall, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT PNEUMO ABEX LLC SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO ABEX CORPORATION ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS PNEUMO-ABEX CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Pneumo Abex LLC successor in interest to Abex Corporation erroneously sued herein as Pneumo-Abex Corporation's motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' false representation and intentional tort causes of action and claim for punitive damages are denied. As to summary judgment, defendant failed to sustain its burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. McFall was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. By failing to submit verified factually devoid responses with its moving papers, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs objected on the grounds of no verifications. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) Defendant also failed to sustain its burden under the negation of an element prong of Aguilar because defendant did not submit evidence negating an essential element of plaintiff's claim. As to summary adjudication, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting their false representation and intentional tort causes of action and claim for punitive damages. By failing to submit verified factually devoid responses with its moving papers, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs objected on the grounds of no verifications. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-31-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Michelle C. Jackson of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4175), appearing for defendant / moving party Maremont Corporation
J. Bruce Jackson of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (510-844-5500), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Mattie McFall, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT MAREMONT CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Before the hearing the Court issued its tentative ruling orally. Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Maremont Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 2, 5, 6 and 7 and granted as to issues 3, 4 and 8 (false representation, intentional tort and punitive damages). As to summary judgment and issues 1, 2, 5, 6 and 7 of summary adjudication, defendant sustained its initial burden. Mattie McFall's declaration and defendant's interrogatory responses create a triable issue whether Mr. McFall was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Grizzly brakes) attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to Mattie McFall's declaration are overruled. As to summary adjudication of issues 3, 4 and 8, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant is liable under causes of action for false representation and intentional tort and for punitive damages. The court considered defendant's Notice of Errata because plaintiffs had notice of defendant's intent to include the verifications a month before their opposition was due and opportunity to respond. (See, e.g., Weiss v. Chevron, U.S.A., Inc. (1988) 204 Cal.App.3d 1094.) Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied. Defendant's request for a continuance is overruled. Counsel for plaintiff to prepare an order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

Off Calendar Mar-25-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on defendant Bentley Motors, Inc., Mercedes-Benz USA LLC and Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.'s Motion to Quash Deposition Notices For Plaintiffs And, In The Alternative, For A Protective Order To Prohibit The Deposition Of Any Plaintiff Until Plaintiffs Provide Required Discovery
Dept. 611, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Joe Vickstein, #4780.

Wes Wagnon, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Mattie McFall, Marcel McFall & Michael McFall.
Garret Sanderson III, Esq. appeared for defendants Bentley Motors, Inc., Mercedes-Benz USA LLC and Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.

At the outset of the hearing, both plaintiffs' & defendants' counsel agree that defendant's motion to quash is moot, but defendant's motion for a protective order is in dispute.

After hearing argument by counsel, and good cause appearing, the Court rules as follows:

Defendants' motion for a protective order to prohibit the deposition of any plaintiff until plaintiffs provide the required discovery is GRANTED.

Counsel for defendant shall prepare the Order After Hearing and submit to the Court for review.
